segments.text: If a man divorces his wife and she leaves him and marries another man, should he return to her again?
Would not the land be completely defiled? But you have lived as a prostitute with many lovers— would you now return to me?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Look up to the barren heights and see. Is there any place where you have not been ravished? By the roadside you sat waiting for lovers, sat like a nomad in the desert. You have defiled the land with your prostitution and wickedness.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Therefore the showers have been withheld, and no spring rains have fallen. Yet you have the brazen look of a prostitute; you refuse to blush with shame.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: My Father, my friend from my youth,
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: will you always be angry? Will your wrath continue forever?
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: This is how you talk, but you do all the evil you can.
During the reign of King Josiah, the Lord said to me,
Have you seen what faithless Israel has done? She has gone up on every high hill and under every spreading tree and has committed adultery there.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I thought that after she had done all this she would return to me but she did not, and her unfaithful sister Judah saw it.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I gave faithless Israel her certificate of divorce and sent her away because of all her adulteries. Yet I saw that her unfaithful sister Judah had no fear; she also went out and committed adultery.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Because Israel's immorality mattered so little to her, she defiled the land and committed adultery with stone and wood.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: In spite of all this, her unfaithful sister Judah did not return to me with all her heart, but only in pretense,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Faithless Israel is more righteous than unfaithful Judah.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Go, proclaim this message toward the north:
Return, faithless Israel, I will frown on you no longer, for I am faithful, I will not be angry forever.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Only acknowledge your guilt— you have rebelled against the Lord your God, you have scattered your favors to foreign gods under every spreading tree, and have not obeyed me,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Return, faithless people, for I am your husband. I will choose you—one from a town and two from a clan—and bring you to Zion.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then I will give you shepherds after my own heart, who will lead you with knowledge and understanding.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: In those days, when your numbers have increased greatly in the land, people will no longer say, 'The ark of the covenant of the Lord.' It will never enter their minds or be remembered; it will not be missed, nor will another one be made.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: At that time they will call Jerusalem The Throne of the Lord, and all nations will gather in Jerusalem to honor the name of the Lord. No longer will they follow the stubbornness of their evil hearts.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: In those days the people of Judah will join the people of Israel, and together they will come from a northern land to the land I gave your ancestors as an inheritance.

I myself said, 'How gladly would I treat you like my children and give you a pleasant land, the most beautiful inheritance of any nation.' I thought you would call me 'Father' and not turn away from following me.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But like a woman unfaithful to her husband, so you, Israel, have been unfaithful to me,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: A cry is heard on the barren heights, the weeping and pleading of the people of Israel, because they have perverted their ways and have forgotten the Lord their God.
Return, faithless people; I will cure you of backsliding.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Yes, we will come to you, for you are the Lord our God.
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: Surely the idolatrous commotion on the hills and mountains is a deception; surely in the Lord our God is the salvation of Israel.
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: From our youth shameful gods have consumed the fruits of our ancestors' labor— their flocks and herds, their sons and daughters.
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: Let us lie down in our shame, and let our disgrace cover us. We have sinned against the Lord our God, both we and our ancestors; from our youth till this day we have not obeyed the Lord our God.
segments.speaker: Israel
